inline_comments.message:
We should use [parameterized logging](https://logback.qos.ch/manual/architecture.html#parametrized) here, which ensures that `getGhostNodeCount()` is lazily executed and is thus only invoked when the log level is `<= info`:

```java
LOGGER.info("Ghost Node Count: {}", getGhostNodeCount());
```
I don't think this comment is adding anything here. I would suggest to remove it

Why are we overwriting the global `cArgs.nodeTypes` with some individual `nodeType`?
I don't think it is a good idea to have some class level mutable state here

I would suggest to call this as `logger.info("DupeTool called with these params: [{}]", cArgs.toString())` directly here and get rid of these meaningless `String msg = "";` and `msg = "...";` assignments before that

General comment: Such comments are always an indication that the code following may actually be better put inside a method ;)

```

You can write lines 248 - 253 just as:

```java
List<String> dependentNodeTypes = obj.getDependentOn().stream().toList();
```
Also can you use `graph1` instead of `gt1` here? If `gt` stands for anything else than `graph` (`graphtransaction`?!) then please use that instead
Please use `For` instead of `4` here for peak professionalism :D
`logger` is registered on the class level, so there is no need to pass it around via method parameters
Please use the string literal here directly instead of assigning it to a `msg` variable first
Please use the string literal here directly instead of assigning it to a `msg` variable first
Here too, also parameterised of course
`logger` is registered on the class level, so there is no need to pass it around via method parameters
Please fix the logger passing for all methods in this class
Please adjust this `msg = ""`; logger.info(msg);` pattern everywhere in this class
Typically we also have the year in this line
In the other script you changed (fixed) this to use `System.exit(statusCode);`
It would probably make sense to refactor this in the sense that you can put lines `157 - 164` inside a `getGraphConfig()` method and call that here and in `getListOfIndexes`
We can be more restful here be mapping this under `/scripts`

Please make all `@Autowired` fields `private final` and annotate the class with lomboks `@RequiredArgsConstructor`.
That makes the class more immutable and would allow better testing (if we actually did that here) since the classes' deps could be passed in via the constructor
You can use `@RequestBody DupeToolRequest` here to avoid the manual String parsing via ObjectMapper and corresponding validation.
You can use lomboks `@Data` here to get rid of the boilerplate getters and setters
Same comment here about private final and requiredargsconstructor
Can we make all of this `List<Map<String, String>>`? Or what can actually be inside `Object` here?

It is always a good idea to register `Pattern.compile` on the class level since it is a rather expensive operation in terms of performance.
Especially here, where you are invoking it for every line in a file
You can use `@RequestBody DataGroomingRequest` here to avoid the manual String parsing via ObjectMapper and corresponding validation.
I was overriding to print actual node type handled in that thread but updated code to print the thread node type without altering cArgs
Removed the section as it wasnt required
There is a JanusGraph instance & then a graph instance so I have updated there names to janusGraph, graph1 & graph2 (1 & 2 because both are different GraphTransaction instances)
I have kept value of Map as Object because it can contain a String or int so to accommodate different types of Objects I have kept it
You can replace this constructor with the `@RequiredArgsConstructor` annotation
